summary: Recorded 12 formal Worker slugs and 9 observed integration drifts.
Loaded 12 hash-pinned Worker Definitions at Backend startup, rejected literal credential fields, and verified host plus container health.
Built agent-cloud/runner-codex-cli:latest without a registry cache importer; Codex CLI 0.144.1 ran as runner and no mock, loopal, or do-agent sidecars were present.
Recorded six real image/binary smoke results, four adapter unit-test results, the Cursor runtime-tree root-cause fix, and the Aider Debian apt 502 block. No Worker support status changed.

Rejected the ignored local Loopal binary because it contains runner/internal/agents/mockagent symbols and fails the formal version-probe contract. The build now requires an explicit non-mock LOOPAL_BINARY.

Built agent-cloud/runner-do-agent:latest from the staged Do Agent binary; version 0.2.7, runner user, configuration directory, Runner probe, and Runner adapter unit test passed. No product support claim changed.
Built agent-cloud/runner-openclaw:latest; OpenClaw 2026.6.11, runner user, configuration directory, and Runner probe passed. Database registration and product gates remain unverified.
Hermes image build exhausted eight bounded python3-pip installation attempts because Debian apt repeatedly returned HTTP 502. No fallback artifact was accepted.

Synced all 12 formal Definitions into the live development agents table, repaired Claude Code and MiniMax drift, created Hermes/OpenClaw, removed mutable runtime image environment injection, and verified the authenticated Worker creation catalog. Only Codex CLI, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI are selectable because they alone have published immutable digest locks.

Verified the live Worker wizard with the local development account. Codex reached server preflight without a create request; Claude and Gemini visibly blocked when no compatible model resource exists; all nine missing-image Worker types were visibly unavailable. Fixed the generated Core proto field-loss root cause and the WASM build publication race, then reloaded the wizard during and after a successful build without a module-not-found overlay.

Created local Worker 7-standalone-1ee3bdcc through the four-step wizard, observed Web and Relay connected, acquired the control lease, sent a minimal ACP prompt, and observed READY. PodService TerminatePod returned Pod terminated; ListPods reported completed and the browser showed no active execution. No secret was read or recorded.

Playwright Chromium ran the formal Worker wizard end to end with test-local UI login. It created Codex, acquired control, received READY from ACP, and terminated its own Worker in afterEach. Codex is verified in local development only; no remote deployment claim was made.

Playwright Chromium logged in through the UI, selected Gemini, verified the missing-model-resource error and disabled next step, and verified all nine missing-image Worker labels. Gemini remains blocked until a real compatible resource is supplied.

Compiled public documentation from all 12 formal Definitions, immutable runtime locks, and evidence matrix; replaced AgentPod and Mesh navigation with Worker pages; verified legacy redirects, all catalog entries, and 390px responsive layout in Chromium. The public docs do not claim undefined, blocked, or unverified types as supported.

Persisted Runner tunnel events through the gRPC adapter and exposed them through Connect, generated WASM/cache projections, and the Runner detail page. A live Gemini Runner exposed stale active-cache reads after the database write; a red service test reproduced the mismatch, then the active cache was atomically synchronized. PostgreSQL, authenticated binary GetRunner, and Chromium all agreed on connected state and 2026-07-12T22:24:18Z. Gemini model execution remains blocked by the absent authorized Google model resource; no fake credential or endpoint was used.

Registry pulls proved all three enabled immutable runtime references are not found. Documentation now reports zero formal deployments, but backend options still trust the stale enabled bit and coordinator/Kubernetes use separate mutable or mismatched image mappings. Reopened the runtime release gate to establish one verifiable source before further Worker promotion.

Disabled all unpullable official digests, validated managed K8s images against the catalog, and generated a local-only catalog from real Codex and Gemini image IDs. Standard dev startup built only those two runners; Chromium created Codex, acquired control, received ACP READY, and terminated it, while Gemini visibly blocked without a compatible model resource and all ten unavailable Worker types were disabled. Official deployable count remains zero.

Inspected the real local Gemini CLI 0.50.0 bundle and confirmed GEMINI_API_KEY is the Gemini API credential while GOOGLE_API_KEY is Vertex AI Express. The platform gemini provider targets the Gemini API, so Definition version 2, AgentFile, legacy provider mapping, and create-time injection now use GEMINI_API_KEY. The local image re-probe passed; Vertex is not claimed as supported.

A real OpenClaw container proved that its formal bare command enters interactive onboarding and exits without a TTY, so the Worker remains blocked. Removed stale Anthropic/Gemini paths for OpenClaw/Hermes so Backend, legacy model selection, and Web filtering match their OpenAI-compatible Definitions. Synced all 12 Definition projections through the documented development command; Chromium again selected Gemini and verified its real missing-model-resource guard. No Worker release state changed.

The active evidence-rebuild plan and state had diverged from the canonical loop manifest and generated control files. Reconciled the control plane to the evidence-rebuild identity, added deterministic manifest/task/monitoring/state consistency checks, and removed the stale duplicate manifest. The durable token estimate already equals the configured 720000 cap, so the loop exited as budget_exhausted before selecting another Worker task. No Worker support state changed.

Restarted the Worker evidence loop after the maintainer request. The control plane now contains all 13 formal Worker types, and seedance-expert has its own generated run. The local worker-definition-sync command synced 13 database projections; the deterministic database verifier matched every formal Definition to its projection without changing Worker support status.

Replaced stale 12-type and historical product claims with the current 13-type runtime matrix. Verified a temporary Runner registration, certificate issuance, mTLS gRPC stream, Relay tunnel, and API/database cleanup without a provider call. Backend gRPC startup now fails fast; development token, Runner quota, seed replay, and IPv4 loopback shadow checks were repaired. No Worker was promoted: browser, provider-backed lifecycle, and full product-path evidence remain pending.

Ran authenticated live PreflightWorker requests for all six selectable Worker types after separating metadata resolution from credential decryption. Codex CLI, Do Agent, MiniMax CLI, and OpenClaw resolved with zero issues. Gemini correctly blocked on the incompatible OpenAI-compatible resource because no Gemini resource is configured. Seedance correctly blocked because its required video resource is disabled. No Pod create endpoint or provider request was used. Five orphaned Pods predate this run and were left untouched.

Chrome DevTools could not create an isolated page for /dev-org/workers/new because its transport closed. No browser, console, network, or screenshot evidence was produced. This block is not replaced by API or unit-test evidence.

Rebuilt Aider and Hermes from their formal Docker targets after replacing the proxy-fragile apt-installed Python dependency with an explicit Python 3.11 runtime plus SQLite library. Both passed isolated no-network Python, SQLite, CLI, and Runner smoke checks. The generated no-network version probe passed for twelve current local Worker tags. Loopal failed its declared --version probe and its binary contains the E2E mock symbol, so it remains blocked. No Worker was promoted; local image facts do not prove an immutable release, matching online Runner, credential injection, provider interaction, Pod lifecycle, or browser path.

Completed the source-grounded architecture review before any create-form or public API change. Physical Runner selection remains a scheduler result and source_expert_id remains Expert-import lineage. The required structural repair is to replace untyped config_bundle_ids with Definition document-ID bindings so format and target_path survive validation, compilation, and run materialization. The proposed architecture also separates Definition semantics, release image state, Runner capability, WorkerSpec intent, and run-manifest facts. Product confirmation is required before implementing this breaking contract migration.

A documentation verifier failed because it asserted retired direct-wizard wording against the current Resource-native Worker contract. Source inspection confirmed the document already distinguishes Resource-native, internal Direct WorkerSpec, and legacy External REST paths and explicitly forbids fallback. Updated only the stale verifier assertions to test the current contract; the verifier, shell syntax check, generated 13-type catalog check, and runtime-status checks pass.

The full Web test suite ran 285 files and failed in three untracked Workflow tests outside this Worker change. WorkflowRevisionDialog.test imports a source file that does not exist. The endpoint-data test expects no create/update/delete endpoints while current source lists them. The WorkflowHeader test expects a new-revision action while current source renders direct edit. The failures were recorded without deleting tests, weakening assertions, or modifying unrelated Workflow code.

Authenticated read-only session APIs and five active development Runner containers were reconciled. Codex, Do Agent/Seedance, Gemini, MiniMax, and OpenClaw report matching harness slugs and executable versions, but every Runner reports a development build and the gRPC protocol carries only slug, version, and path. The Backend create-option selector tests only slug availability. Adapter ID, interaction modes, and runtime image digest are not attested, so all six selectable Worker matrix entries were explicitly downgraded to slug-presence-only evidence. No Pod, credential, or provider action occurred and no Worker was promoted.

Definition primary-model protocol adapters now flow through the Worker create options protobuf, backend mapper, Web DTO, and Resource-native model-resource hook. Regression tests proved Do Agent and Seedance exclude MiniMax, and a live authenticated Connect options request returned Do Agent's exact openai-compatible and anthropic adapters without reading provider credentials or creating a Pod. Chrome DevTools remained unavailable, and low disk space blocked full Go package linking and WASM rebuild. No Worker support state changed.

Removed the config-bundle silent drop path. Config-kind EnvBundles now require a JSON object in __json during Create and Update, and existing malformed rows cause Pod command construction to fail instead of making USE_CONFIG_BUNDLE a no-op. A live authenticated Connect CreateEnvBundle request for malformed JSON returned 400 invalid_argument before persistence. Definition config_documents remain structurally unbound from config_bundle_ids, so this is not a configuration-document migration or a Worker support promotion.

Compared the authenticated live ListWorkerCreateOptions response with all 13 on-disk Definitions. Every formal Worker type was present and its primary-model protocol adapters plus interaction modes matched exactly. Only Codex CLI was selectable in this current environment. This remains API projection and slug-level Runner availability evidence only; no browser, provider, Pod lifecycle, or Worker support state changed.

Invalidated prior live API evidence against :10000 because it belongs to another worktree stack. Rebuilt the real workspace baseline at :12400: only Codex has a verified local runtime catalog image and only Codex plus e2e-echo Runners are online. Repaired the local catalog generator and Runner service mapping so all 13 formal Worker types can be included after Docker confirms their image IDs. Node tests, runtime catalog shell contract, runner runtime contract, shell syntax, and diff checks passed. Docker image construction is paused because another worktree Buildx job reduced free disk to 188 MiB; no Worker was promoted.

Started the real Aider compose build in this workspace after disk recovered to 129GiB. It reached Buildx bake initialization but emitted no Dockerfile layer for 180 seconds while another worktree Buildx build remained active. The local queued request was cancelled only after confirming no Runner container existed. This is an environment scheduling block, not an Aider build result; no support claim changed.

A focused Dockerfile/Compose audit found that runner-aider runs the pip-based Aider installer on the default Node base because Compose did not pass RUNTIME_BASE=python-runtime-base. Added a red/green Compose contract and explicit build argument. Dockerfile contract, Compose configuration, Runner runtime contract, local catalog tests, and diff check pass. No Docker build was claimed because the external Buildx queue remains active; no Worker support state changed.

Removed the overlapping Next.js Connect proxy rewrite and verified local login plus the browser Worker Template form. Browser rendering exposed all 13 Worker types and their explicit image blocks. A red/green contract repair added cursor-cli to the Runner entrypoint allowlist. A full MCP Registry development import caused a large failing database write that starved Runner readiness and heartbeats, so development now writes and exports MCP_REGISTRY_ENABLED=false; the restarted Backend process confirmed the variable and logged no new sync. Post-restart API and database checks still timed out while an unrelated worktree Docker build saturated the shared Docker VM. No image build, Runner stability, Worker lifecycle, provider credential, or Worker support claim changed.

A red/green browser-backed accessibility repair gave revision inputs, placement policy, resource allocation, and custom CPU/memory/storage/GPU controls explicit label targets. The real Worker Template route loaded after a clean reload, ListWorkerCreateOptions returned 200, the DOM contained all 13 formal Worker types with only Codex selectable and 12 explicit missing-image blocks, no HTML labels were unassociated, and the console had no errors or issues. Docker services and the Codex Runner were stable after the external queue cleared. No provider credential, Pod lifecycle action, image build result, or Worker support promotion occurred.

Built the Aider image with the repaired python-runtime-base, verified aider 0.86.2 under the runner user, regenerated the local immutable-image catalog, and restarted the official local Backend/Relay lifecycle. The dev-runner-aider container established mTLS plus tunnel, reported aider 0.86.2, and held three heartbeat intervals. The Backend returned Aider as selectable and the real Web form selected Aider's image plus PTY mode while retaining 13 types and explicit blocks. No Pod, terminal data plane, provider credential, or provider request occurred; Aider remains not_supported.

Built Cursor CLI from the upstream installer, verified agent 2026.07.09-a3815c0 under the runner user, regenerated the local immutable-image catalog, and restarted the official local Backend/Relay lifecycle. The dev-runner-cursor container established mTLS plus tunnel, reported cursor-cli, and held three heartbeat intervals. The real Web form exposed Cursor as selectable, selected its local image, and limited interaction modes to PTY and ACP with ACP selected. The first lifecycle retry failed because the amesh protoc plugin received signal 9, but a standalone reproduction used 45.6MB maximum RSS and succeeded; the unmodified official retry succeeded. No Pod, terminal data plane, provider credential, or provider request occurred; Cursor remains not_supported.

Built and probed Gemini CLI 0.50.0, regenerated the official local catalog, and observed dev-runner-gemini online with a connected tunnel and repeated heartbeats. The live Web template selected Gemini, its local image, ACP by default, and PTY/ACP modes; it now marks ModelBinding required from the live Worker option contract. Read-only database evidence proved the only visible binding qa-primary-model targets openai-compatible gpt-5 while Gemini requires gemini, so the generic ModelBinding catalog exposes an incompatible candidate that the backend only rejects later. No Pod, credential read, provider request, or terminal action occurred. The compatibility query requires product confirmation before implementation; Gemini remains not_supported.

Built Claude Code, verified claude 2.1.211 under the runner user, regenerated the official local catalog, and observed dev-runner-claude online with a connected tunnel and repeated heartbeats. The real Web template exposed Claude as selectable, selected its local image and ACP mode, and marked ModelBinding required. The same qa-primary-model binding targets openai-compatible gpt-5 while Claude requires anthropic, so the generic ModelBinding catalog remains an unresolved architecture-boundary defect. No Pod, credential read, provider request, or terminal action occurred; Claude Code remains not_supported.

The repaired official dev lifecycle started Backend, Marketplace, Relay, Gitea initialization, and the DoAgent Runner. The image probe returned do-agent 0.2.7; the Runner reported do-agent and seedance-expert, maintained a connected tunnel, and continued heartbeats. In the real browser, a DoAgent WorkerTemplate selected the local image, ACP, ModelBinding qa-primary-model, ComputeTarget local-runner-pool, and ResourceProfile standard-profile; validation, plan, and apply produced WorkerSpec snapshot 2. A Worker invocation then validated and planned against that immutable template. The Worker plan was intentionally not applied because actual launch may use the configured provider credential and no named non-production invocation authorization exists. Database evidence confirms zero Worker resources, launches, and new DoAgent Pods. DoAgent remains not_supported.

The shared DoAgent image and dev-runner-do-agent were live: Seedance Expert was selectable with ACP and requires the seedance-video tool model (doubao, openai-compatible, video-generation). The browser WorkerTemplate plan correctly blocked because no ToolBinding or compatible Doubao video model resource exists. A deterministic defect collapsed the valid hyphenated role path to /spec; a red/green Go test and browser retry repaired it to /spec/toolModelResourceIds/seedance-video. The organization AI resource UI exposes encrypted Doubao API-key configuration and video/video-generation model registration. No credentials were written or read, no external provider request occurred, and database counts confirm zero Seedance Pods, Worker launches, and templates. Seedance Expert remains not_supported. The 24-iteration loop limit is reached without extension.
